messages.text: happy new year everyone! hope you all had a restful break. excited to kick off 2025 together 🎉
welcome back team! quick reminder — the office fridge got cleaned out over the break so label your stuff. also we have bagels in the kitchen to celebrate the new year.
dude did you watch the playoff game last night? that fourth quarter comeback was insane
yes!! I was screaming at my TV lol. we should grab beers and watch the next one
does anyone know the wifi password for the small conf room on the 3rd floor? I keep getting kicked off
it's BsusGuest2024 — they haven't rotated it yet. should be on the whiteboard in there too
hey quinn, can we push our 2pm sync to 3pm today? I have a dentist appointment that's running late
no worries, 3pm works. hope the dentist isn't too painful 😬
bob have you tried that new ramen place on 5th? I went over the break and it was incredible
no but I've been meaning to! want to go for lunch today? I'm craving something warm, it's freezing out
yes let's do it. 12:30?

hey team, kicking off infra-migration this week. high-level plan: move everything off the legacy EC2 fleet onto AWS EKS. I've been drafting the Terraform configs for the new cluster — 3 node groups, one for stateless services, one for the DB proxy layer, one for batch jobs. @charlie_ross I need you to start converting our deploy scripts into helm charts, and @bob_kim we need to figure out the DB migration step in the CI/CD pipeline so the schema changes run before the new pods come up.

alright team, kicking off design-system-v2 this week. iris and I have been auditing the existing component library and there's a lot of inconsistency — we need a single source of truth for design tokens, starting with the color palette and spacing scale. iris will own the Figma side, nina will bridge to implementation. let's get aligned in tomorrow's sync.

hey mike, I started scoping out the OAuth2 authorization code flow with PKCE for auth-service. the current implementation skips the code_verifier entirely which is a pretty big gap. gonna draft a design doc today — want to co-own the review?

sounds good — I'll start by converting the api-gateway deploy config into a helm chart this week. question: are we doing one helm chart per service or a monorepo umbrella chart? I'm leaning toward per-service so each team can own their own values.yaml and we don't get merge conflicts on every deploy

yeah absolutely, PKCE is critical especially since we're supporting public clients now. I'll loop in @karen_wu for the crypto side — she had opinions on the code_challenge_method (S256 vs plain) last time we discussed identity provider config. also want to make sure the login endpoint redirect flow still works with the new button variants eve is building in design-system-v2

per-service helm charts 100%. each service gets its own chart with a values.yaml for staging and production overrides. I'll set up the base chart template with the standard k8s configs — Deployment, Service, Ingress, HPA. you can extend from there for the api-gateway specifics like the load balancer annotations

heads up team — kicking off the auth-refactor sprint today. main goals: 1) implement PKCE in the OAuth2 flow 2) add refresh token rotation to prevent token replay attacks 3) migrate session data to the new session_tokens table. @bob_kim can you start on the schema migration for session_tokens? we need columns for token_hash, user_id, issued_at, expires_at, and revoked_at

on it — quick question: for the session_tokens table, should we index on (user_id, expires_at) or (token_hash)? leaning toward a composite index on user_id + expires_at so we can efficiently purge expired sessions, plus a unique index on token_hash for lookups

for the DB migration step — I'm thinking we run it as a Kubernetes Job that executes before the main deployment rolls out. basically an init container pattern: the migration job runs, verifies schema is at the expected version, then the pods start. that way we don't get the app trying to read columns that don't exist yet

both — we need the token_hash unique index for the auth middleware to validate tokens on every request, and the composite for the cleanup cron. also add a device_fingerprint column, @karen_wu mentioned we might want device-bound sessions later for the identity provider integration

exactly what I was thinking. use a pre-install/pre-upgrade helm hook for the migration job. the tricky part is rollback — if the migration fails, we need the job to exit non-zero so helm aborts the release. can you prototype this in the staging environment first? I'll get the EKS cluster provisioned via terraform this week so you have something to deploy to

hey nina, I started restructuring the Figma file for design-system-v2. I'm setting up a dedicated page for semantic colors — brand, surface, text, border tokens. do you have a preference for how we name them in tokens.json? I want to make sure the Figma variable names map 1:1 to what you use in code.

great question — I've been using a flat namespace like color.text.primary, color.surface.default, color.border.subtle in tokens.json. if you can mirror that hierarchy in the Figma variables, the export to code will be seamless. I'll set up the Style Dictionary config to auto-generate CSS custom properties and JS constants from tokens.json.

infra-migration kickoff: we're moving off EC2 onto AWS EKS. I'm standing up the Kubernetes cluster with Terraform this week — 3 node groups, managed node pools with auto-scaling. @charlie_ross is converting deploy configs to helm charts, @bob_kim is building the DB migration step into the CI/CD pipeline as a k8s Job. targeting staging environment by end of Jan. will share the terraform plan output once the EKS module is ready for review

started refactoring the login endpoint to support the new OAuth2 flow. right now /api/auth/login just does basic credential check + returns a JWT. gonna split it into /api/auth/authorize (starts PKCE flow) and /api/auth/token (exchanges code for access + refresh tokens). any concerns?

looks right @charlie_ross — make sure the /api/auth/token endpoint validates the code_verifier against the stored code_challenge. and we need to set the access token expiry to 15min and refresh token to 7 days per @karen_wu's security guidelines doc

saw the infra-migration announcement — how are you handling secrets management in the new EKS cluster? we have a bunch of DB credentials and API keys that are currently in env vars on the EC2 boxes. want to make sure the auth-service secrets are properly rotated during the migration

good question — I'm setting up AWS Secrets Manager with the Kubernetes External Secrets Operator. secrets get synced into k8s Secret resources automatically and the pods mount them as env vars. rotation happens in Secrets Manager and the operator picks up the new values without a redeploy. I'll make sure the auth-service secrets are in there — can you send me the list of keys it needs?

hey eve, wanted to sync on the frontend side of auth-refactor. the login endpoint is getting split into authorize + token exchange endpoints. for the login page, we need to generate a code_verifier on the client side, hash it for the code_challenge, and send it with the initial authorize request. also — are you pulling the button styles from tokens.json yet? I want the login button to use the new primary variant from design-system-v2

yep I saw charlie's message. I'll update the login flow to handle the PKCE challenge — gonna use the Web Crypto API for the code_verifier generation. re: design-system-v2, I'm waiting on @nina_scott's storybook PR to merge before I can pull from tokens.json, but I can hardcode the primary button variant for now and swap it out after

heads up — I'm restructuring the storybook setup for design-system-v2. going to organize stories by atomic design levels: atoms (Button, Icon, Badge), molecules (FormField, Card), organisms (Navbar, Sidebar). each story will document accessibility props and WCAG compliance status.
lisa did you see that new motion design reel on dribbble? the micro-interactions are so smooth. sending you the link
omg yes that was gorgeous. the easing curves on the card transitions are exactly what I want to learn. thanks for sharing!

migration for the session_tokens table is ready for review: adds token_hash (varchar 64, unique), user_id (uuid, FK to users), issued_at, expires_at, revoked_at (nullable), device_fingerprint (varchar 255, nullable), and the composite index on (user_id, expires_at). PR is up — #auth-342

nice, reviewing now. one thing — can we add a scope column to session_tokens? we're going to need per-session scopes for the OAuth2 consent flow so the access token only gets the scopes the user approved
good call, I'll add a scopes column as jsonb — that way we can store the array of granted scopes without a join table. pushing the update now

first helm chart draft is up for the api-gateway: includes the Deployment with readiness/liveness probes, a Service of type ClusterIP, and an Ingress resource with ALB annotations for the load balancer. @dave_patel can you review the ingress config? not sure if I have the right annotations for the AWS ALB controller vs the nginx ingress controller we discussed

eve, should I start on the Button component first? I saw iris has some variants spec'd out in Figma — primary, secondary, ghost, and destructive. want to make sure I'm building against the right specs before I write the React code.

question on the auth middleware — when we validate the access token JWT, should we also check if the corresponding session in session_tokens has been revoked? that adds a DB call per request but it means we can instantly revoke sessions instead of waiting for token expiry

yes, button is the highest priority. nina is syncing with iris on the final token values today. hold off until tokens.json has the semantic color definitions landed — you'll want to reference those directly instead of hardcoding hex values. the whole point of the design token layer is that components consume tokens, not raw colors.

reviewing now. we're using the AWS Load Balancer Controller, so the ingress annotations need to be alb.ingress.kubernetes.io/* not the nginx ones. I'll push a fix to the helm chart — also need to add the HPA config, we want the api-gateway to scale from 2 to 10 pods based on CPU utilization at 70%

yes, we need revocation checks in the auth middleware. I know the DB hit is a concern — let's add a Redis cache layer. cache the session status with a short TTL (30s) so we get near-instant revocation without hammering the session_tokens table. @karen_wu had a pattern for this in the security playbook, let me dig it up
heads up — Config 2025 early bird tickets go on sale tomorrow. if anyone wants to go, let me know and I'll get approval for the team budget

auth-refactor update: we're splitting the login endpoint into OAuth2-compliant authorize + token endpoints with PKCE. frontend login flow will generate code_verifier client-side. session data moves to a new session_tokens table with proper scopes support. @karen_wu will review the token rotation strategy before we ship to staging. targeting end of week for the backend pieces

got it, swapping the annotations now. for the HPA — should we also add a memory-based scaling metric? the api-gateway can be memory-heavy when it's caching response payloads. I've seen the pods OOMKill at 512Mi under load
yes add memory too — set requests at 256Mi and limits at 768Mi, with HPA targeting 75% memory utilization as a secondary metric. we'll tune these once we have the Datadog metrics flowing in staging

thanks for the heads up mike. I'll block time thursday to review the token rotation implementation. main thing I want to verify: when a refresh token is used, the old one must be immediately invalidated in session_tokens — if we detect reuse of an already-rotated refresh token, we need to revoke the entire session family. that's the standard refresh token rotation security pattern

hey bob, for the token exchange endpoint I need to store the authorization code temporarily before it gets exchanged. should I add an auth_codes table or just use Redis with a 10min TTL? the code maps to (code_challenge, redirect_uri, scopes, user_id)

oscar, I've been mapping out the UX states for all button variants — default, hover, pressed, focused, disabled. for the focused state, WCAG 2.4.7 requires a visible focus indicator. I'm proposing a 2px offset ring using the color.focus.ring token. want me to add this to the Figma component specs?

Redis with TTL makes more sense — auth codes are ephemeral by design and we don't need to query them after exchange. less migration churn too. just make sure you delete the key immediately after successful exchange, don't just rely on TTL
yes please. also make sure the focus ring has enough contrast against all surface colors — we need WCAG AA minimum (3:1 for non-text elements). iris can validate the token values in Figma.

starting work on the frontend auth flow changes for auth-refactor. the login page will now do a PKCE authorize → redirect → token exchange flow instead of the current simple POST to /api/auth/login. I'll need to store the code_verifier in sessionStorage between the redirect. @mike_torres does auth-service return the tokens as httpOnly cookies or in the response body?

@eve_johnson access token in response body (stored in memory, never localStorage), refresh token as httpOnly secure cookie with SameSite=Strict. that way the access token lives only in the JS runtime and the refresh token can't be accessed by scripts. @karen_wu approved this pattern in the security review last quarter

makes sense. I'll set up an auth context provider that holds the access token in state and handles silent refresh when the token expiry hits. the refresh will POST to auth-service /api/auth/token with grant_type=refresh_token and the httpOnly cookie goes along automatically

heads up — I'm adding a DB migration step to the infra-migration CI/CD pipeline. it runs as a Kubernetes Job via a helm pre-upgrade hook before the main deployment. if you have services that depend on specific schema versions, let me know and I'll add the migration ordering to the pipeline. @alice_chen this will affect the session_tokens migration from auth-refactor too

PR #auth-345 is up — implements the /api/auth/authorize endpoint with PKCE support in auth-service. generates the authorization code, stores code_challenge + scopes in Redis, and redirects to the callback URL. @charlie_ross can you review since you're building the token exchange side?

just pushed the initial tokens.json to the design-system-v2 branch — it has the base color palette (primitives) and the first set of semantic aliases for text, surface, and border tokens. the Figma variables iris set up map directly to these. next step is wiring these into the Button and Typography components in storybook.

reviewing now. quick thing — I see you're using SHA256 for the code_challenge but the code_challenge_method isn't being stored alongside. we need to persist that so the token endpoint knows how to verify the code_verifier. I'll add a comment on the PR

the terraform plan for the EKS cluster is done — 3 managed node groups, t3.large for stateless, r5.large for the DB proxy, t3.medium for batch. VPC is shared with the existing infra so the RDS instances stay reachable during migration. going to run terraform apply on the staging environment tonight after hours. PR is up for review

oscar, when should I start on the motion tokens? I want to define standard easing curves and durations for the component library — things like transition.duration.fast (150ms), transition.duration.normal (250ms), and the easing functions. I can also start prototyping Lottie animations for loading states.

good catch, fixed. storing code_challenge_method in the Redis payload now. also added validation to reject 'plain' method — we should only support S256 per the RFC. @karen_wu flagged this as a hard requirement

looked at the terraform plan output — the VPC peering config looks solid. one question: the security group for the DB proxy nodes only allows ingress from the stateless node group CIDR. do we also need to allow the batch node group? some of the batch jobs run direct DB queries for data backfills

let's get the color and spacing tokens stabilized first, then you can layer in motion. probably week 3. but go ahead and start documenting the motion token spec in Figma — durations, easing curves, which component states get transitions. that way we're ready to implement when the time comes.
good catch — I'll add the batch node group CIDR to the security group ingress rules. updating the terraform config now

session_tokens migration merged. running it on staging now. also added a background job to purge expired sessions — runs every hour, deletes rows where expires_at < NOW() and revoked_at IS NOT NULL. should keep the table from growing unbounded

nice. one tweak — the purge job should also delete rows where expires_at < NOW() even if revoked_at IS NULL. those are sessions that naturally expired. right now you're only cleaning up explicitly revoked ones
ah you're right, fixing that. should be expires_at < NOW() regardless of revoked_at

hey eve, heads up — the login page error states need to handle new OAuth2 error codes from auth-service (invalid_grant, invalid_client, etc). can you map those to user-friendly messages? also I want the error toast component to use the new semantic colors from design-system-v2 — @iris_wang finalized the error/warning/success palette last week and it's in tokens.json under semantic.feedback
anyone want to grab coffee? I'm doing a starbucks run in 10 min
oat milk latte please! I'll venmo you
same for me please, medium drip. thanks charlie!
on it. I'll create an error mapping util in the auth module. for the design tokens — is tokens.json published to npm yet or should I import from the monorepo directly?

token exchange endpoint (/api/auth/token) PR is up — #auth-348. handles authorization_code grant type with PKCE verification, issues JWT access token (15min expiry) + refresh token (7 day expiry), stores session in session_tokens table. @alice_chen @karen_wu would love eyes on the token generation logic specifically

EKS cluster is live on staging. 3 node groups, all healthy. kubectl access is configured through aws-auth ConfigMap — @charlie_ross @bob_kim I've added your IAM roles, you should be able to kubectl get pods now. next step: deploy the api-gateway helm chart and the DB migration job to staging and see if the whole pipeline works end to end

published the v1 color palette in Figma for design-system-v2. the semantic color tokens are organized as: text (primary, secondary, tertiary, disabled, inverse), surface (default, raised, sunken, overlay), border (default, subtle, strong), and interactive states. @nina_scott these should match what's in tokens.json — let me know if anything's off.
confirmed, I can see the nodes. deploying the api-gateway helm chart to the staging namespace now

reviewing. the JWT payload looks good — sub, iss, exp, iat, scopes. one thing: we should add a jti (JWT ID) claim that maps to the session_tokens row so we can tie the access token back to its session for revocation checks in the auth middleware

just cross-checked — the Figma variables line up with tokens.json perfectly. one thing: can you add color.interactive.primary-hover and color.interactive.primary-pressed? I need those for the Button component's hover and active states. grace is blocked on the React implementation until those exist.
smart, adding jti now. I'll use the session_tokens.id as the jti value so the auth middleware can do a single lookup by PK instead of scanning by token_hash

api-gateway pods are up but the ingress isn't getting an external IP. the ALB isn't provisioning — I think the AWS Load Balancer Controller isn't installed on the cluster yet. can you check if the helm release for it is deployed in kube-system?
on it — I'll derive them from the primary base. -hover will be 8% darker, -pressed will be 12% darker. I'll update the Figma variables and you can pull into tokens.json.
ah yeah I forgot to include the AWS LB Controller in the terraform module — adding it now as a helm release in the EKS add-ons. should be up in 10 min after I run terraform apply
LB controller is deployed. try again
ALB is up and the ingress has an external DNS now. I can hit the api-gateway health endpoint through the load balancer. staging is looking good

reviewed #auth-348. the PKCE verification looks correct. two concerns: 1) the refresh token should be stored as a SHA256 hash in session_tokens, not plaintext — if the DB is compromised we don't want usable refresh tokens leaking. 2) add a token_family column to session_tokens so we can implement refresh token rotation with family-based revocation
nina, do we have the spacing scale locked down yet? I'm building the Button component and need to know the internal padding. is it the 4px base scale? so 4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, 48?

yep, 4px base. it's in tokens.json under spacing.* — spacing.xs is 4px, spacing.sm is 8px, spacing.md is 16px, spacing.lg is 24px, spacing.xl is 32px. button padding should be spacing.sm horizontal and spacing.xs vertical for size=small, spacing.md/spacing.sm for medium.

great catches. hashing the refresh token makes total sense — I'll SHA256 it before storing and compare hashes on refresh. for token_family, do we generate a new family UUID on initial login and carry it forward through rotations?

exactly. new family UUID on login. when a refresh token is used, issue a new one in the same family and revoke the old one. if a revoked refresh token in the family is ever reused, revoke ALL tokens in that family — it means the token was stolen. this is the standard token rotation theft detection pattern from the OAuth2 security BCP

saw karen's feedback on token_family — I'll add that column to the session_tokens migration. uuid type, indexed, nullable for backward compat with existing sessions. should have the migration up in an hour
thanks bob, that's perfect
sofia do you have the zoom link for the all-hands tomorrow? I can't find the calendar invite
just forwarded it to you. it's the same recurring link we always use — I'll pin it in #general too

the DB migration job ran successfully on staging — schema is at version 47 which matches what we need for the current api-gateway release. the helm pre-upgrade hook pattern works: the job runs, exits 0, then the deployment rolls out. tested a failing migration too and helm correctly aborted the release
nice work. how long does the migration job take to run? I want to make sure it doesn't time out in the CI/CD pipeline — GitHub Actions has a 6-hour limit but our deploy timeout is set to 10 minutes

auth middleware refactor PR is up — #auth-351. the middleware now: 1) extracts JWT from Authorization header, 2) verifies signature + expiry, 3) checks jti against session_tokens to see if session is revoked (with Redis cache, 30s TTL), 4) attaches user + scopes to request context. @karen_wu would appreciate a security review when you have a chance

currently takes about 45 seconds on staging. the slow part is the connection pool warming up against RDS — I've tuned the pool size to 5 and the migration runs faster now. worst case for a big schema change is maybe 2 minutes. well within the 10-minute timeout
lgtm on the middleware approach. question — how are we handling scope enforcement? does each route define required scopes and the middleware checks the JWT scopes array against it?

yeah exactly — I added a requireScopes() decorator that routes can use. e.g. @requireScopes(['user:read', 'user:write']) on a controller method. the auth middleware populates req.scopes from the JWT and the decorator checks the intersection. clean separation between authn and authz

auth context provider PR is up for the frontend auth-refactor work. handles: storing access token in memory (React state), silent refresh via /api/auth/token with refresh_token grant type, automatic retry of failed requests after refresh, and redirect to login on 401. lmk if anyone wants to review

nice work. does the silent refresh kick in before the access token expiry or after? we should refresh proactively — like when there's 2min left on the 15min token expiry — so the user never sees a 401. also, make sure the loading state on the login button matches the new interaction patterns from design-system-v2. @grace_park added a loading spinner variant to the Button component last week

good call, I'll add a timer that triggers refresh when exp - now < 120s. for the button, I'll switch to the design-system-v2 Button once grace's PR is merged — right now I'm using a local loading state that just disables the button

infra-migration staging update: EKS cluster is running, api-gateway is deployed via helm chart with ALB ingress, DB migration job runs as a pre-upgrade hook, and the full CI/CD pipeline works end to end through GitHub Actions. next up: setting up Datadog monitoring for the k8s cluster — I want pod-level metrics, node health, and APM traces before we start migrating production traffic

refresh token rotation is implemented in auth-service. when POST /api/auth/token with grant_type=refresh_token comes in: 1) hash the incoming refresh token, 2) look up in session_tokens, 3) if found and not revoked → issue new access + refresh tokens, revoke old refresh token, same token_family. 4) if found but already revoked → REVOKE ALL tokens in that family (theft detection per @karen_wu's guidance). PR #auth-355

this looks great. one edge case — what happens if the refresh token has expired (past its 7-day window)? we should return an expired_token error and force re-login rather than just generic invalid_grant

great progress on infra-migration. question — for the production cutover, are we planning a blue/green deployment or a rolling migration? I want to make sure the auth-service endpoints stay available during the transition, zero-downtime is non-negotiable for login flows

opened a PR for the Button component — supports primary, secondary, ghost, and destructive variants with three sizes. all consuming design tokens from tokens.json. storybook stories included. @eve_johnson can you review when you get a chance? I want to make sure the accessibility props and aria attributes look right.

good point, adding a specific check for that. will return { error: 'expired_token', error_description: 'refresh token has expired' } so the frontend can distinguish between theft revocation and natural expiry

zero-downtime deployment is the plan — we're going to run both the old EC2 fleet and the new EKS cluster behind the same load balancer with weighted routing. start at 10% traffic to EKS, monitor the Datadog dashboard for error rates and latency, then ramp up gradually. if anything goes wrong we shift 100% back to EC2 instantly. the auth-service will be one of the last services we migrate since it's highest risk

mid-sprint auth-refactor update: backend OAuth2 flow with PKCE is mostly complete — authorize, token exchange, and refresh token rotation are all in review. auth middleware now validates JWTs against session_tokens with Redis caching. frontend auth context provider is in progress. main risks: identity provider integration hasn't started yet, and we need @karen_wu's final security sign-off on the token rotation theft detection logic before merging to main

bob, quick question on the session_tokens table — what's the estimated row count after 30 days if we assume 10k DAU each with ~3 sessions? want to make sure the purge job + indexes can handle the volume
grace have you ever tried that korean bbq place near the office? a bunch of us are going friday after work
oh I love that place! count me in. who else is going?
so far it's me, lisa, oscar, and frank. the more the merrier though — invite whoever

rough math: 10k users × 3 sessions × 30 days = 900k rows at peak, but the hourly purge job removes expired ones so steady state should be closer to 10k × 3 = 30k active sessions. the composite index on (user_id, expires_at) will handle that easily. I'll add a pg_stat monitor to Datadog just in case

looking at the PR now. the button variants look great, but I noticed the disabled state doesn't have aria-disabled — you're using the HTML disabled attribute which removes the button from tab order. for accessibility, we should keep it focusable but non-interactive. also the contrast ratio on the secondary ghost variant is only 3.8:1 against surface.default, which fails WCAG AA (needs 4.5:1 for text).
good catches — I'll switch to aria-disabled and intercept click events. for the contrast issue, should I flag it to iris to adjust the ghost variant token or bump it on our side?
flag it to iris — the token values need to be correct at the source. we shouldn't override design tokens in component code. that defeats the whole purpose of the token system.

iris, eve flagged a WCAG contrast issue — the ghost button's text color (color.interactive.secondary) against surface.default is only 3.8:1. we need 4.5:1 minimum for AA compliance. can you darken color.interactive.secondary in the Figma palette? I'll update tokens.json once you've verified it passes contrast checks.
ugh, good catch. I ran it through the Figma contrast plugin and you're right. bumping from #6B7280 to #4B5563 gets us to 5.2:1 which clears AA. updating the variable now.

iris just updated color.interactive.secondary in Figma — I've ported the new value to tokens.json. contrast ratio is now 5.2:1 against surface.default, clears WCAG AA. you can pull latest and the ghost button variant should be good.
pulled and verified. looks great, merging the Button PR now. 🎉

hey charlie, the frontend needs to handle the PKCE flow across redirects. when we call /api/auth/authorize, auth-service redirects to the identity provider and then back to our callback URL with the auth code. where does the identity provider redirect go exactly? is it /api/auth/callback on the backend or a frontend route?

I've got the GitHub Actions workflow for the helm chart deploys working. on merge to main it builds the Docker image, pushes to ECR, updates the image tag in values.yaml, and runs helm upgrade on the staging cluster. want me to add a manual approval gate before it hits production?

I've uploaded the typography scale spec to the design-system-v2 Figma file. heading-xl through body-sm, with line-height and letter-spacing tokens for each. @iris_wang can you verify the font weights map to our brand font's available weights? I want to avoid faux-bold rendering issues.
shout out to the eng team for crushing it on the year-end deploy. zero incidents over the holiday break. that's how it's done 🙌

it goes to a frontend route — /auth/callback. the frontend extracts the authorization code from the URL params, then POSTs to /api/auth/token with the code + code_verifier from sessionStorage. that way the code_verifier never leaves the browser
makes sense, thanks

yes, definitely need the manual approval gate for production deploys. also add a terraform plan step that runs on PRs so we can see infra changes before they're applied. the GitHub Actions workflow should output the terraform plan diff as a PR comment
on it
checked — we have 400, 500, 600, and 700 weights available. your spec uses 350 for body text which doesn't exist. suggest bumping to 400 (regular). everything else maps cleanly.
ah, my mistake. updated to 400. thanks!

mike, I'm thinking about the identity provider integration. right now auth-service handles everything internally but we need to support external identity providers (Google, Okta) for enterprise SSO. should I build an adapter pattern in auth-service so we can swap between internal auth and external IdP? that way the OAuth2 flow stays the same but the identity verification step is pluggable

yes, adapter pattern is the right call. each identity provider implements a common interface: verifyCredentials(), getUserProfile(), revokeExternalSession(). then auth-service picks the right adapter based on the tenant config. @karen_wu should weigh in on the security implications of delegating identity verification to external IdPs — specifically around token trust boundaries

starting on the identity provider adapter interface for auth-service. will support internal auth + external IdPs (Google, Okta). the authorize endpoint will route to the correct IdP based on tenant config. @charlie_ross this will change how the authorization code is generated — for external IdPs the code comes from them, for internal auth we generate it ourselves. let me know if this impacts the token exchange flow

hmm yeah — for external IdPs the token exchange needs to happen in two steps: first exchange the external IdP's code for their tokens, then issue our own JWT access + refresh tokens stored in session_tokens. PKCE would be between us and the external IdP in that case. I'll refactor the token endpoint to handle both internal and external grant flows

for the IdP adapter — do we need a new table to store IdP configs per tenant? thinking something like identity_providers (tenant_id, provider_type, client_id, client_secret_encrypted, discovery_url, scopes). happy to draft the migration
